---
title: "Venue landing page"
description: "Show a hero image, your story, and opening hours above the menu. Optional but makes a great first impression."
sidebarTitle: "Landing page"
icon: "window"
---

The landing page is an optional intro section that appears above the menu
on your public URL. Use it to introduce your venue before the diner sees
the food — a hero image, a short story, your hours, whatever fits.

## Where to build it

In your workspace, go to **Settings → Landing page**. You'll see a block
editor with a hero image slot and content blocks underneath.

## What goes on a landing page

<Columns cols={2}>
  <Card title="Hero image" icon="image">
    A full-width banner at the top. Upload your own, pick from the
    [gallery](/vendors/media/gallery), or generate one with AI.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Venue name and description" icon="align-left">
    A short intro paragraph. AI can draft this from your venue details.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Owner message" icon="message">
    A personal note from you to your diners. AI can draft a friendly
    version.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Hours" icon="clock">
    A readable version of your opening hours.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Content sections" icon="rectangle-list">
    Add text-and-image sections for whatever else you want to say — the
    story, the chef, the awards, anything.
  </Card>
</Columns>

## Publishing the landing page

The landing page has its own publish flow, separate from the menu:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Edit">
    Make your changes in the editor. They're saved as a working copy and
    aren't visible to diners yet.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Preview">
    Add `?landingPreview=1` to your public URL while signed in as owner
    or staff to see the working landing page above your menu.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Publish">
    Tap **Publish** in the landing editor. The new layout appears above
    the menu on the next scan.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Skipping the landing page

If you leave the hero slot empty and don't add any content blocks, the
public URL skips straight to the menu. There's no "empty landing shell"
showing up for diners.

<Tip>
If you just want a tight, menu-first experience, skip the landing page
entirely. The QR menu experience is designed to work great without it.
</Tip>

## AI helpers

Rumizi can draft landing content for you:

- **Generate description** — writes a short venue intro.
- **Generate owner message** — writes a friendly welcome note.
- **Generate section text** — drafts text for a content block based on
  your topic.
- **Generate hero image** — creates a banner image from your venue
  details.
- **Improve hero image** — upgrades an existing banner photo.
- **Generate logo** — creates a simple logo mark if you don't have one.

All AI output is editable — you're never stuck with what it suggests.
